content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Michael Anderson 
The Council for Political 
Responsiveness 
2261 Market street, Ste. 225 
San Francisco, 
CA 
94114-1693 
Dear Mr. Anderson: 
July 14, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-87-162 
You have requested advice concerning the disclosure provisions 
of the Political Reform Act.1J 
QUESTION 
Is the Council for Political Responsiveness (CPR) required to 
file disclosure reports under the provisions of the Political 
Reform Act? 
CONCLUSION 
Based on the materials enclosed with your letter, it appears 
that CPR merely provides information about and encourages 
participation in the political process. 
If that is the case, CPR 
has no filing obligations under the Act. 
However, your letter 
provides no specific details about CPR's activities, and therefore 
it is not possible to provide you with a definite conclusion. 
The 
information contained in the analysis section of this letter 
should help you in determining whether CPR is required to file 
disclosure reports. 
FACTS 
According to the brochure enclosed with your letter, CPR is a, 
non-partisan, non-profit community organization whose purpose is: 
... to promote and support the principle that each citizen 
is equally responsible for voting on public issues, 
assisting in the development of governmental policy and 
maintaining the integrity of the persons in offices of 
authority. 
1JGovernment Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box H07 • 
S;:Jcr:mwntd CA QC;R()4A1R()7 .. (Qlf.) 1??~';AAl) 


Michael Anderson 
July 14, 1987 
Page 2 
\: : 
The brochure also states that CPR's specific programs include: 
• 
Generating increased participation in public voting 
through media campaigns and special promotions. 
• 
Providing the public with effective, concise, factual 
information on significant governmental actions and 
policy issues. 
• 
Inception and continual augmentation of a pUblic-servant-
accountability-system that is easily accessible to the 
average person. 
• 
Recording and publishing a model-system-manual for 
start-up and operation of similar organizations in other 
communities. 
• 
Providing guidelines and activities for public access of 
the legislative body that most concerns them in order to 
help the public create positive change or input an 
effective voice in the process of policy-making. 
• 
Promoting increased awareness in the significance of each 
person's vote and each person's correspondence to 
legislators on issues. 
In addition to the brochure, you provided a "CPR Infosheet" of 
pUblications which are available to interested persons from state 
and federal government agencies as well as other politically 
active organizations. 
ANALYSIS 
As stated previously, your letter does not provide any 
specific details about CPR's activities and how it accomplishes 
its purposes. 
Therefore, I hope the following general information 
will help you to determine whether CPR's activities will create 
disclosure obligations under the Act. 
The Act requires a membership organization like CPR to file 
canpaign disclosure reports if it receives $500 in campaign 
contributions during a calendar year. 
Membership dues are 
"ccntributions" to CPR if CPR uses them for the purpose of making 
contributions to or expenditures on behalf of state or local 
officeholders, candidates or committees. 
(Sections 82013(a), 
84100-84217.) 
Enclosed is a copy of the 1987 "Information Manual 
on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act" 
which you should use to determine CPR's filing obligations if $500 
in a calendar year is spent to support or oppose officeholders, 
candidates or committees. 


Michael Anderson 
July 14, 1987 
Page 3 
In addition, organizations which employ a lobbyist or a 
lobbying firm for the purpose of attempting to influence state 
legislative or administrative actions incur filing obligations 
under the lobbying disclosure provisions of the Act. 
(Sections 
86100-86300.) 
Organizations which do not employ a lobbyist or a 
lobbying firm, but which spend $5,000 in a calendar quarter (other 
than campaign contributions) to influence state legislative or 
administrative actions also must file lobbying disclosure reports. 
(Section 86115.) 
Enclosed is a copy of the 1987 "Information 
Manual on Lobbying Disclosure Provisions of the political Reform 
Act" which you should use to determine CPR'S filing obligations if 
money is being spent to influence legislative or administrative 
actions. 
I hope the foregoing has been helpful. 
If you need further 
assistance with regard to CPR's possible filing obligations, 
please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
By:

Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

We are writing to request an official Letter of Determinatil 
indicating whether our organization: CPR/qualifies as a 
"Political Committee" under your terms. 
We would like to maintain a close and positive relationship 
with the FPPC in the future and wanted to confirm that our 
filings were in order. 
Thank you. 
Sincerely,

CPR

FOR POLITICAL

THE COUNCIL FOR POLITICAL RESPONSIVENESS 
2261 Market Street

San Francisco, CA 94114-1693 


SOME THOUGHTS ••• 
A journey of a thousand miles begins with the first step. 
-Old Turkish proverb 
A government has something more to do than to govern, and 
levy taxes to pay the Eovernors. It is something more than 9 
police to arrest evil ana punish wrong. It must also encoumge 
good, point out improvements, open roads of prosperity and 
infuse life into all right enterprises. It should combine the best 
minds of the state for all the high ends for which society is 
established and to which man aspires. That gives us much 10 
do. -Joshua Chamberlain 
, am only one, but still I am one. I cannot do everything, but 
still I can do something; And because I cannot do everything, I 
will not refuse to do the something that I can do. 
- Edward Everett Horton 
Ye shall know the truth and the truth shall make you free. 
- John 8;32 (Engraved in marble at CIA headquarters entrance) 
In a gentle way, you can shake the world. - Mahatma Gandi 
There are no great (people) in this world only great challenges 
which ordinary (people) rise to meet. - Admiral Bill Halsey 
Perhaps the most valuable result of all education is the ability 
to make yourself do the thing you have to do, when it has to be 
done, whether you like it or not. This is the first lesson to be 
learned. - Thomas Henry Huxley 
The rights of children are worth fighting for, because children 
are our best known source of adults. -Shari Lewis 
... patriotism is not so much protecting the land of our fathers as 
preserving the land of our children. - Jose Ortega y Gasset 
Better light a candle than curse the darkness. 
-Old Chinese proverb 
Truth cannot be told so as to be understood without being 
believed. -William Blake 
Ambition often puts men upon doing the meanest offices, so, 
climbing is performed in the same posture with creeping. 
- Jonathan Swift 
I believe that it is better to tell the truth than a lie. I believe it is 
better to be free than a slave. And I believe it is better to know 
than to be ignorant. - H. L. Mencken 
Without rules there can be no game, without discipline there 
can be no freedom, and without responsibility there can be no 
rights. - Walter Hoving 
This above all: to thine own self be true, And it must follow, as 
the night the day, Thou canst not then be false to any man. 
-Shakespeare 
)
